pain: Unable to add new auto-generated sites to the platform account for over a week. Multiple support tickets filed without resolution. Blocks delivery of client work across multiple projects.

Claude reports that it is pushing to Website Studio but the site does not actually update. Diagnosed in session as a file-structure issue — Claude is dumping output into a non-target directory, but the failure mode is silent from the user-facing perspective. Blocks hub site delivery for the roofing client.

Fully switched site production from Website Studio to hand-rolled HTML/CSS via Warp + Claude Code after repeated inconsistencies. Built two additional large sites with zero hiccups using Claude Code, called it "life-changing." Signals that the Studio is not the preferred tool for multi-page production among advanced members.

Output generated locally in Claude looks good, but when the same prompts run through the SA MCP / Website Studio pipeline, design quality degrades. Member proposed allowing HTML/zip upload of locally-built sites to SA backend as a workaround.
Wants ability to attach a persistent design brain (markdown + rendered HTML) so the SA platform has consistent design context across generations. Currently must re-establish context every run.
Reports successful use for location-service landing pages (3 prompts → done). Open problem: connecting landing pages to root domain for SEO. Uses Claude Code for anything beyond a single page.
4 of 5 sites built came out well; one had unresolved design consistency issues. Wants the build process optimized for design consistency.

Confirmed on outdated V1 endpoint (mcp.searchatlas.com/api/v1/mcp). V2 URL shared in chat did not establish connection. V2 carries GSC specifics, keyword rankings, custom reports, Google Ads, local/GBP reports, data exports — critical for their 530-customer workload.

Hitting rate limits on multiple endpoints (some at 100/day). With 530 customers and report generation workflows, limits are hit within hours. No per-endpoint rate limit documentation currently available; no clear upgrade path for higher-volume agencies.

Public MCP documentation is incomplete — schemas and tool descriptions are missing, causing LLMs to produce confused/incorrect tool calls. Member built schemas internally to work around it and offered to submit a PR to the docs.

Built a custom Claude skill (in-house) that opens Chrome and manually hyperlinks pages — $30–$40/day in Claude tokens, runs for hours. Wants SA MCP to expose an internal-linking workflow: audit → plan → execute, respecting neighborhood rules, orphan detection, and cannibalization cleanup.

Atlas Brain is too slow for their workflow — 45-minute sessions. Reports the team "revolted" over the V3 UI rollout, preferred V2. V2 deprecation banner caused internal panic. Atlas Brain V2 shipping this/next week should help.

Avoids Content Genius due to speed/latency. Built their own AI content writer trained on their existing content (~$1/article). Would use SA path if they could: write content externally → push to SA → receive content score back via MCP.

Runs two brands under one leadership with two separate SA accounts. Currently maintains their own authorization layer between SA MCP and the two-brand setup. Would benefit from native multi-brand routing in the MCP.

Members request ability to upload locally-built HTML/zip of a site to SA backend, so SA publishes the member-generated site. Current gap: once locally built, there is no path back into the SA ecosystem. Buy-in from Bryan Fikes and Justin Day as an immediate feature request.

Group-wide blocker: Website Studio output varies dramatically between runs for the same prompts. SA dev team acknowledged the issue. Workaround observed: members switching to Warp + Claude Code HTML/CSS generation and abandoning Studio for multi-page work.

Group-wide root cause of Website Studio push failures traced to wrong target directory / broken file structure on the member side. The failure is silent — Claude reports success while pushes fail. SA implementing vault-level architecture support; in the meantime, diagnostic visibility in Studio would surface these misconfigurations earlier.

Search Atlas MCP GitHub repo documentation is incomplete. Many MCP tools have no schemas defined, causing LLM agents to get confused about tool signatures and hallucinate usage. Healthcare agency member built their own schemas on their side as a workaround; they have offered to submit PRs. This blocks adoption by technically sophisticated member teams.

Enterprise/healthcare agency with two distinct brands requires authorization layer on top of Search Atlas MCP to route users to the correct of two separate SA accounts. No native multi-account MCP routing exists; member built a custom auth layer. HIPAA compliance makes this non-negotiable for healthcare agencies. Gap in enterprise-tier MCP feature set.

Website Studio MCP-generated sites show inconsistent design quality compared to what Claude produces locally. JD proposed allowing members to inject pre-built HTML/zip as a workaround pending backend fix, but no mechanism currently exists to push locally-built HTML into Website Studio directly. Members fall back to exporting and manual copy-paste.

Account-level limit issues (such as auto-site cap) submitted through standard support queue do not get resolved in reasonable time (week+ wait confirmed with enterprise member). Resolution required direct intervention from SA host rather than support team. Process gap: AMM members currently have no official escalation path distinct from standard support.

AMM team explicitly decided to build a dedicated HTML landing page hosted in Circle (a third-party platform) rather than using Website Studio as the resource hub for members. The team is routing around Website Studio for static HTML hosting — indicating the product cannot serve as a reliable delivery platform for HTML-based content. Confirmed across three planning sessions.

Website Studio AI chat mode is accessible only via the dashboard UI — it cannot be invoked or controlled through MCP write operations. Members who attempt to use MCP to trigger AI chat generation fail silently or receive errors with no clear explanation. The capability is not exposed via the MCP API, creating a mismatch between member expectations (set by demos and announcements) and what the MCP can actually do.

No in-product visibility into credit cost per action before running a playbook. Distinction between auto-site credits (static, non-recurring) and AI generation credits (recurring per billing cycle) is not communicated in the UI. Member hit quota unexpectedly and had no self-service way to understand the credit model or refresh options.

MCP exposes no endpoint to query current credit balance or set per-project credit limits. Members running automated playbook workflows cannot programmatically gate execution on available credit. Manual monitoring is the only option.
